title: Shi Xiao
content:
Overview
[
]

Shi Xiao is a powerful demon general whose cultivation ranks among the top three within the demon general domain. His appearance is distinctly demonic, accompanied by a cold and prideful demeanor, a dark gaze filled with ambition, and a faint, almost mocking smile that hints at his past. His temperament is ruthless and aggressive, shaped heavily by a long history of conflict. One defining event from three hundred years ago was the scar Mo Lihai left on his face—an insult Shi Xiao never forgot and never forgave. This humiliation fueled his violent nature, deep hatred, and his relentless desire to prove his superiority over his rivals.

History
[
]

Over the years, Shi Xiao’s cultivation surged dramatically. By the time of the demon general competition, he had risen to a level comparable to an early-stage Ascendant cultivator, his entire body filled with fierce demonic spiritual energy. This power, combined with his speed, precision, and ruthlessness, made him both respected and feared. His confidence in battle was absolute. During the competition, he declared that Mu Yun was no match for him and demanded that Mu Yun admit inferiority. When Mu Yun unleashed five strands of life-and-death demonic energy, Shi Xiao shattered the attack with a diamond-shaped burst of light, striking Mu Yun directly between the eyebrows and forcing him into a grievous retreat. His speed and destructive techniques left a deep impression on all who witnessed them.

Shi Xiao’s hostility extended especially toward outsiders; he repeatedly stated that while demon generals do not engage in true life-and-death battles with each other, foreigners exist only to be killed. After defeating Mu Yun, he attempted to challenge Mo Lihai, but Mo Lihai refused. Shi Xiao then redirected his killing intent toward Wang Lin, whom he despised. His confrontations with Wang Lin were marked by arrogance, threats, and attempts to intimidate him. Shi Xiao even confronted Wang Lin when Wang Lin broke into his mansion, expressing killing intent and attempting to strike him down. Despite his ferocity, there were moments during these confrontations when he hesitated—revealing that beneath his cruelty was someone capable of fear.

Shi Xiao was also involved in several other encounters. One notable fight occurred when Mu Yun relied on help from Tian Lui of the Giant Spirit Sect. Shi Xiao swiftly defeated Tian Lui with a decisive, surgically precise attack. His connections with other demon generals were strained as well. His relationship with Chen Tao, the senior apprentice brother of Shi Xiao’s junior apprentice brother, was tense and hostile, shaped by past disagreements and differing stances regarding Wang Lin.

Shi Xiao’s ambition was clear: he wanted to dominate the demon general competition and rise to the highest position available to him. His power, reputation, and ruthless presence shaped the political and interpersonal dynamics of the demon general mansion. However, his downfall came from the very hatred he cultivated. When he confronted Wang Lin again, Shi Xiao charged forward with killing intent and prepared a powerful spell. Before he could release it, Wang Lin unleashed a terrifying Yin–Yang shadow technique. A dark shadow surged from the ground, passed through Shi Xiao in an instant, and shattered his life force completely. Shi Xiao collapsed with his eyes still wide open, full of killing intent, but utterly lifeless. Wang Lin stated that he had promised long ago to take Shi Xiao’s life, and this moment fulfilled that vow.

Shi Xiao’s end was swift and absolute. His ambitions, grudges, cruelty, and pride all led him directly to the fatal confrontation he sought. His death marked the conclusion of a violent and turbulent path—one defined by power, hatred, and an unyielding desire to dominate, but ultimately cut short by the one opponent he could never surpass: Wang Lin.

Spells
[
]

Six Finger Demon Sword
